steps.title: Calculate the confidence interval
steps.content:
Margin of error of 0.5 miles means the sample mean can vary by that amount around the true population mean. The confidence interval for the true population mean is:

$$[4.5 - 0.5, 4.5 + 0.5] = [4, 5]$$

The key point: this interval estimates where the population mean (the actual average for all visitors) likely falls, not where individual visitors' distances fall.

steps.title: Identify the correct interpretation
steps.content:
Let me evaluate each choice:
  • A: all visitors hiked 4-5 miles — applies the interval to individual hikers, not the population mean
  • B: most visitors hiked exactly 4.5 miles — the sample mean is an average, not a typical individual value
  • C: not possible any visitor hiked less than 3 miles — incorrectly applies the interval to individuals and uses absolute language
  • D: the mean distance for all visitors is plausible between 4 and 5 miles — correctly identifies that the interval bounds the population mean, and the word plausible matches the probabilistic nature of a confidence interval

Choice D accurately describes what margin of error tells us. That matches choice D — D is correct.
